identitySentence: Hunter Sargent, PLLC is a Denton estate planning and business planning law firm founded in 2021 by a seventh generation Dentonite who has practiced law since 2014.

concept.headline: Seven generations in Denton. One place to plan its future.
concept.subheadline: Planning a will, a trust, or the future of your business can feel overwhelming. At Hunter Sargent, PLLC your first consultation is free, fees are flat, and every plan is built around your family and your work.
credibilityPoints: Seventh-generation Dentonite, returned to Denton in 2014 to practice
Admitted in Texas state courts, the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Texas, and the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Eastern District of Texas

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: Hunter Sargent, PLLC helps Denton families and business owners with estate planning, wills, trusts, probate, and alternatives to probate, including Lady Bird deeds, irrevocable and testamentary trusts, and Medicaid planning. The firm also handles business planning, from LLC, S-corp, and partnership planning to business succession and asset protection planning.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: Hunter Sargent is a seventh-generation Dentonite who returned to Denton in 2014 to practice law and opened his firm here in 2021. The office sits at 319 W Oak St in downtown Denton, serving families and businesses across Denton County.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your initial consultation is free, and the firm works on a flat-fee structure so you know costs up front. You will be asked about your family, your assets, and your goals, and you will get an honest assessment of what planning you actually need.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the team at Hunter Sargent, PLLC,

Your firm opened in Denton in 2021, but the story behind it runs seven generations deep in this county — that kind of local root system is rare, and it deserves an intake process that works as hard after hours as you do during them.

Your website is already one of the stronger ones we have reviewed: clear scheduling links, a working contact form, and strong Denton positioning. What a site like yours cannot do on its own is guarantee that every person who reaches out at 10 p.m. about a will, a Lady Bird deed, or an LLC gets an immediate, thoughtful reply and a confirmed seat on your calendar. That is exactly the gap the IntakeBrief Lead Capturer closes. It is a single, focused tool — not a software platform — built around one job: turning website inquiries into confirmed, deposit-backed consultations.

We have already drafted your firm's custom identity sentence and a contact form tailored to your estate planning and business planning practice. Your identity sentence reads:

"Hunter Sargent, PLLC is a Denton estate planning and business planning law firm founded in 2021 by a seventh generation Dentonite who has practiced law since 2014."

Here is how it works once a potential client submits the form:

- The inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox, with the sender's address in Reply-To so your staff can respond personally at any time.
- The potential client automatically receives a response specific to their message — a probate question gets a probate reply, a business succession question gets a business succession reply. Sorting is rule-based, not guesswork.
- That response presents your genuinely available consultation times.
- A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the time held on your calendar reflects a committed visitor, not a maybe.

We make no revenue guarantees. What we offer instead is proof: a 15-day proof period during which you test the complete experience yourself, end to end, before committing to anything.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information at any point.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to your TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email with no action from your staff.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm the response addresses the actual topic of the inquiry.
11. Confirm that genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter type.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will make it right or walk away.

We would welcome the chance to put this in front of you for 15 days and let the results speak.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
